Name: SJ077166
Clinical Presentation: 3 yr M with ACT, probably a carcinoma
Clingen: POS TP53 variants
CLINGEN COMMENT: The patient is heterozygous for a single base pair substitution, g.12460C>T in exon 5 of the TP53 gene (Genbank NC_000017).  This single base substitution has been previously identified in the TP53 database (http://www-p53.iarc.fr) and is predicted to result in a missense mutation, p.R158C. The patient is also heterozygous for a 16 base pair insertion, g.11258insACCTGGAGGGCTGGGG  (p53PIN3) in intron 3. This is listed in the TP53 database as a known polymorphic variant, but there are some reports that associate it with an increased frequency of some cancers (Oncogene (2004)23,1954-1956; Carcinogenesis (2010)31,643-647; Cancer Investigation (2008)26,168-172). Four additional polymorphisms were identified in this sample.  This sample is heterozygous for g.12273G>A in intron 4, g.12803A>G in intron 6, g.13491C>T in intron 7 and g.13511T>G also in intron 7. All of these changes are listed in the TP53 database as known polymorphic variants.

Name: SJ077280
Clinical Presentation: 4 yr F with ACA for excision
Clingen: No TP53 mutations were identified in the DNA extracted from the patient's peripheral blood, however, the patient was homozygous for a single base pair substitution, g.12139C>G (Genbank X54156) which would be predicted to lead to a missense mutation, P72R.  While this alteration is listed in the database (http://www-p53.iarc.fr) as a known polymorphism, there have been some reports that associate it with an increased frequency of some cancers.
CLINGEN COMMENT: This screening methodology can miss some mutations that  are present in introns or regulatory regions, as well as some deletions any of which could lead to non-functional TP53 proteins.  Therefore, the lack of mutation detection in this screen does not conclusively rule out the possibility of TP53 mutations in this patient. The patient was also homozygous for several other reported polymorphisms: g.11818insC in intron 2, g.14234T>C, g.14235T>C and g.14271C>T in intron 7, and g.17699A>C in intron 10.

Name: SJ033271
Clinical Presentation: 17 yr F with ACC undergoing adrenal mass resection
Clingen: genome-wide ploidy change and large-scale copy number alterations.
CLINGEN COMMENT: Tumor Ploidy The comprehensive sequencing analysis of the patient's tumor sample revealed hypertriploid modal chromosome numbers (n=~73), and the overall findings were suggestive of a genome doubling followed by multiple chromosome losses. Therefore, all numerical abnormalities are expressed in relation to the tetraploid level.     Chromosomal losses:  Whole chromosome/chromosome arm One copy loss (3 copies remain): 1q, 2, 3, 4, 6, 9, 10, 12, 14, 15 and X Two copies loss (2 copies remain): 11, 17, 18, 22 Segmental deletion Segmental deletions across 1p. Segments 1p36.11-p34.2, 1p33-p32.3 and 1p22.2-p12 showed two copy losses and the remaining regions had one copy loss.  Chromosomal gains: No large-scale chromosome gains cnLOH: No large-scale copy neutral LOH
GERMLINE: EPCAM VUS, MSH2 VUS, RECQL4 VUS

Name: SJ030228
Clinical Presentation: 7 yr F with metastatic EWS of the lung for resection
Clingen: EWSR1::FLI1, STAG2 Q925Q, TP53 R248W
CLINGEN COMMENT: Whole genome, whole exome and transcriptome analysis of the patient’s tumor sample is POSITIVE for the pathologic or likely pathologic variants described below.  This tumor specimen has an EWSR1-FLI1 fusion, alterations in TP53 and STAG2, a missense alteration in MAP3K4 and multiple chromosomal abnormalities.  Structural Variants t(11;22)(q24.3;q12.2), EWSR1-FLI1 A translocation event between chromosomes 22 and 11 [chr22:29683979(+)::chr11:128678453(+)] results in an in-frame fusion product, EWSR1-FLI1 (NM_013986 exon 8 fused to NM_002017 exon 8).  Single Nucleotide Variants and Indels MAP3K4K1489E NM_005922:c.4465A>G; p.Lys1489Glu [Allele frequency: 10% WGS,  13% WES, 9% RNA] This is a missense alteration in which an A is replaced by a G at coding nucleotide 4465 and is predicted to change a Lysine to a Glutamic Acid at amino acid codon 1489.  STAG2Q925Q NM_001042749:c.2775G>A, p.Gln925Gln [Allele frequency: 67% WGS,  61% WES, 87% RNA] This is a synonymous alteration in which a G is replaced by an A at coding nucleotide 2775, which is the last coding nucleotide of exon 27.  No coding change is predicted; however, intron 27 retention associated with this event is observed by transcriptome analysis, suggesting that this alteration may have an impact on splicing.   TP53R248W NM_000546:c.742C>T; p.Arg248Trp [Allele frequency: 94% WGS,  93% WES, 99% RNA] This is a missense alteration in which a C is replaced by a T at coding nucleotide 742 and is predicted to change an Arginine to a Tryptophan at amino acid codon 248. This alteration occurs in a region of copy neutral loss of heterozygosity; as such, no wildtype copies of TP53 are predicted to remain in the tumor specimen.  Tumor Ploidy Chromosomal Losses Partial: 16q Chromosomal Gains Full: 6, 8, 12, 14, 15, 19, 20, X Partial: 1q, 9q  There is copy neutral loss of heterozygosity involving chromosomes 5 and 17.
GERMLINE: Analysis of 63 cancer predisposing genes in this patient’s peripheral blood sample reveals a heterozygous variant of uncertain significance in CDK4.  No pathogenic or likely pathogenic variants are detected.

Targeted Sequencing Findings: Whole exome and transcriptome analysis of the patient's tumor sample is POSITIVE for the pathogenic variants described below. This tumor specimen has an EWSR1::FLI1 fusion and a truncating STAG2 variant. STRUCTURAL VARIANT - EWSR1::FLI1 fusion.  t(11;22)(q24.3;q12.2).  chr22:29688160(+)::chr11:128651853(+).  EWSR1 (NM_013986.3) exon 11 is fused in-frame to FLI1 (NM_002017.4) exon 5. Note: This fusion involves a recurrent breakpoint in the FLI1 gene (PMID: 21872822, PMID: 23329308), although the exon 11 of EWSR1 is not a commonly detected breakpoint in Ewing sarcoma (PMID: 10197607, PMID: 20308673, PMID: 31995669). How these variations in the fusion region affect the function of the oncoprotein remains controversial.  SNVI - STAG2 frameshift duplication.  NM_001042749.2: c.1876dup; p.Thr626AsnfsTer9.  13% WES, ~17% RNA.  Note: The single nucleotide duplication within exon 20 of STAG2 results in a frameshift of the protein coding sequence (beginning at amino acid 626) and creates a premature stop codon after 9 new amino acids, which would be expected to reduce or abrogate the activity of this protein product. Truncating STAG2 variants have been reported in Ewing sarcoma (PMID: 34129824, PMID: 25223734, PMID: 25186949).

Name: SJ032958
Clinical Presentation: 10 mth with small round cell sarcoma with CIC gene rearrangement of the kidney for nephrectomy
Discrepant Information: not discrepant, Dx name change
RNASeq: Transcriptome (RNA-seq) analysis of this patient's tumor specimen is POSITIVE for a CIC-activating translocation. This translocation [t(10;19)(q26.3;q13.2); chr10:135480000(+)::chr19:42808895(-)] directly involves the first intron of PRR19 (NM_199285.2) on chromosome 19 and an intergenic region located approximately 600 bp upstream from the 5’ end of the DUX4 gene  (NM_033178.4)  on chromosome 10. The chromosome 10 rearrangement brings DUX4 into close proximity to CIC. The breakpoint of this translocation on chromosome 19 is approximately 9 kb upstream from the 3' end of CIC (NM_015125.4). Even though the CIC gene is not structurally affected by this translocation, this structural rearrangement alters the regulatory environment of CIC and results in transcriptional upregulation of the gene, as demonstrated by increased RNA expression of CIC and its downstream targets, ETV4, ETV5, ETV1, and CCND2. In summary, the translocation detected in this sample does not represent exactly a CIC rearrangement  because the structure of the CIC gene is not affected by this event.  Though not a CIC rearrangement per se, this translocation markedly enhances the CIC transcriptional activity in this tumor, and from a clinical standpoint, it would be expected to have a similar functional effect as a CIC structural rearrangement. Of note, the CIC FISH probe used in the analysis of this tumor specimen (FC-21-00529) includes the genomic sequence upstream (centromeric) to the CIC gene, which is involved in the 19q13.2 rearrangement that was detected by RNA sequencing in this sample. This probe design explains the positive result obtained by the CIC FISH assay

Name: SJ058433
Clinical Presentation: 16 yr M with hx of ATRT and low-grade MPNST, now progressing MPNST on the back
Clingen: LARGE-SCALE DNA COPY NUMBER VARIANTS - Chromosomal losses:  1p34.3-p21.3, chromosome 4, chromosome 10, chromosome 13, 16p11.2-q24.3, 19q13.13-q13.43. Chromosomal gains:  No large-scale chromosomal gains
CLINGEN COMMENT: No classification can be provided for this tumor by methylation profiling. This can be observed for rare tumors that are not represented in the reference cohort or in samples with low tumor purity. This sample was deemed of adequate tumor purity per pathologist review
Targeted Sequencing Findings: Whole exome and transcriptome analysis of the patient's tumor sample is POSITIVE for the pathogenic variants described below. This tumor specimen has alterations in BRAF, NF1, KDM6A, and SMARCB1. SNVI - BRAF  V600E.  NM_004333.4: c.1799T>A; p.Val600Glu.  35% WES, 40% RNA.   NF1 W1314*.  NM_000267.3: c.3941G>A; p.Trp1314Ter.  76% WES, 81% RNA.   KDM6A  R1351*.   NM_021140.3: c.4051C>T; p.Arg1351Ter.  82% WES, 74% RNA. SMARCB1 frameshift deletion.  NM_003073.4: c.642del; p.Pro215LeufsTer14.  33% WES, 23% RNA. SMARCB1 splice.  NM_003073.4: c.500+1G>A; p.?   41% WES, 81% RNA. NF1 and Ras-MAPK pathway alterations have been reported in malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumors (PMID: 29118384). The single nucleotide substitution (C>T) within exon 28 of KDM6A replaces the amino acid arginine with a premature stop codon at position 1351 (post-JmjC region, which is essential for its catalytic activity). This would be anticipated to result in an abnormal protein product with reduced or abrogated functionality. The H3K27-demethylase KDM6A interacts with other chromatin-modifying protein complexes and catalyzes the removal of di- and trimethyl groups on histone H3 lysine 27, thereby promoting target gene activation (PMID: 24561908). Somatic loss-of-function variants in KDM6A have been identified in several cancer types, but it has not been reported in MPNST (cancer.sanger.ac.uk/cosmic). Malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumors have been reported  in the setting of rhabdoid tumor predisposition syndrome  (PMID: 29512865).

The H3K27-demethylase KDM6A interacts with other chromatin-modifying protein complexes and catalyzes the removal of di- and trimethyl groups on histone H3 lysine 27, thereby promoting target gene activation (PMID: 24561908). Somatic loss-of-function variants in KDM6A have been identified in several cancer types, but it has not been reported in MPNST (cancer.sanger.ac.uk/cosmic). Malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumors have been reported  in the setting of rhabdoid tumor predisposition syndrome  (PMID: 29512865).

Notes: 90% of tumor - METASTATIC NEUROBLASTOMA. IMMUNOSTAINS ARE POSITIVE FOR SYNAPTOPHYSIN, NEGATIVE FOR neuN AND GFAP. The specimen is received fresh labeled, """"""""left temporal brain tumor"""""""" and  consists of multiple pieces of red-brown soft tissue measuring 2.6 x 1.9 x 0.6 cm in aggregate. A normal karyotype was detected in 15/18 metaphases analyzed; three metaphases, which were unsuitable for complete analysis showed hyperdiploidy.  FISH evaluation of this specimen following hybridization  with LSI N-myc probe detected three to four copies of chromosome 2 in 92% of the cells examined.  The combined results of karyotype and FISH analyses, therefore, are indicative of the presence of hyperdiploid karyotype, which  is seen in a subset of neuroblastomas.

Name: SJ031914
Clinical Presentation: 2 yr M with hx of leukemia and new dx of adrenal mass, stage L1, no prior treatment
INPC: Unfavorable histology; 18 months up to less than 5 years old
CLINGEN COMMENT: Molecular Cytogenetics: FISH using the NMYC probe with 2q11 as the control revealed the NMYC gene was NOT amplified in the 200 interphase nuclei from the touch prep. Gain of 1 to 2 signals for NMYC was found in (~57%).
RNASeq: TCF3-PBX1 and ETV6-RUNX1 fusion transcripts were NOT DETECTED in this patient’s sample.
Targeted Sequencing Findings: Whole genome, whole exome and transcriptome analysis of the patient's tumor sample is POSITIVE for the pathologic and likely pathologic variants described below. This tumor specimen has alterations in NRAS, PTPN11, PAX5 and LEF1.  SNVI - NRAS Q61R   NM_002524.4: c.182A>G; p.Gln61Arg   8% WGS, 9% WES, 7% RNA.  PTPN11 F71L   NM_002834.4: c.213T>A; p.Phe71Leu   5% WGS, 6% WES, 5% RNA.  PAX5 G367S   NM_016734.2: c.1099G>A; p.Gly367Ser   14% WGS, 15% WES, 4% RNA.  CNV - LEF1   DEL   1 copy loss exons 2 – 3   NM_016269.5    4q25(109062696_109088423)x1~2.  Note: One copy gain of chromosome 4 was detected by WGS   Tumor Ploidy - Chromosome number: 55~57. Category: High hyperdiploidy. Chromosomal losses: No large-scale chromosome losses. Chromosomal gains: 1q, 4, 6, 8 (sub-clonal), 10, 14, 17, 18, 21 (tetrasomy), X and Y(sub-clonal).  cnLOH: 1p, 9, 13 and 15
GERMLINE: This tumor specimen has a deletion in 11q, 17q gain, a sub-clonal deletion in 1p36 and other segmental copy number alterations across the genome.

Clinical Presentation: 21 mth F, stage 4, N-MYC not amplified, post treatment, for resection
INPC: favorable histology: less than 18 months old
Targeted Sequencing Findings: Whole genome, whole exome and transcriptome analysis of the patient’s tumor sample is POSITIVE for the multiple chromosomal gains described below.  No reportable structural, SNV/Indel or copy number variants were detected.  Tumor Ploidy - Chromosomal Losses  No large scale chromosomal losses.   Chromosomal Gains  Full: 1 2 4 5 6 7 8 9 10 12 13 16 17 18 20 Partial: 15q 22q
GERMLINE: This addendum report supersedes SG-16-00072 (4/27/2016) and updates the interpretation of the variant described below. The change in variant classification was made as a result of re-review of the evidence based on updated variant interpretation guidelines and/or new information.   NF1   NM_000267.3:c.7127-19_7127-8delGTTTGTTTGTTT    23% WGS, 50% WES   Heterozygous   Uncertain Significance   Benign.  Interpretation Summary - Pathogenic variants in NF1 are associated with Neurofibromatosis type 1, a rare autosomal dominant disorder characterized by café-au-lait macules, iris Lisch nodules, axillary and inguinal freckling, and an increased risk for the development of neurofibromas and optic pathway gliomas (OMIM ID: 162200).  The NF1 c.7127-19_7127-8delGTTTGTTTGTTT change has a maximum subpopulation frequency of 3.38% in gnomAD v2.1.1 including 6 homozygotes (https://gnomad.broadinstitute.org/). Algorithms that predict the impact of sequence changes on splicing indicate that this variant does not affect splicing. To our knowledge, this variant has not been reported in individuals with Neurofibromatosis type 1.  In summary, this variant meets criteria to be classified as benign.

Name: SJ030645
Clinical Presentation: 8 yr M with hx of OS of the femur with limb sparing surgery, now with lung mets
Targeted Sequencing Findings: Whole genome, whole exome and transcriptome analysis of the patient's tumor sample is POSITIVE for the pathologic and likely pathologic variants described below.  This tumor specimen has a complex karyotype including amplification of 17p11.2, TP53 disruption and a two copy deletion of TUSC7. STRUCTURAL VARIANTS - TP53 disruption   t(6;17)(p22.3;p13.1)   chr17:7590694(-)::chr6:15452237(+).  A translocation event between chromosomes 6 and 17 results in the disruption of TP53 in intron 1. It is unclear if a wildtype copy of TP53 remains in the tumor. Expression of TP53 is observed by transcriptome analysis, which may represent retained wildtype TP53 in the tumor or background normal cell expression.  COPY NUMBER VARIANTS - TUSC7   DEL   2 copy loss   NR_015391   3q13.31(116302245_116574025)x0.  Tumor Ploidy - This specimen has a highly complex karyotype, with multiple areas of gains, losses, chromothripsis and over 500 copy number and structural variants. Of note, there is amplification of 17p11.2, which has been previously reported in high-grade osteosarcoma [van Dartel et al. Cancer Genet Cytogenet 2002;139(2):91-6].

This is a missense alteration in which a C is replaced by a G at coding nucleotide 364 and is predicted to change an Arginine to a Glycine at amino acid codon 122.  Classification Criteria   PM2 – Moderate.  Absent from controls (or at extremely low frequency if recessive) in Exome Sequencing Project, 1000 Genomes Project, or Exome Aggregation Consortium [Variant present at 0.002% in ExAC]. BP4 – Supporting Multiple lines of computational evidence suggest no impact on gene or gene product (conservation, evolutionary, splicing impact, etc.).   MSH6 intronic   NM_000179:c.3557-3_3557-2insT   30% WGS, 27% WES.  This is an intronic alteration in which a T is inserted in intron 6, two nucleotides upstream of exon 7. This alteration adds an additional T nucleotide into a run of T repeats.  Classification Criteria  PM2 – Moderate.  Absent from controls (or at extremely low frequency if recessive) in Exome Sequencing Project, 1000 Genomes Project, or Exome Aggregation Consortium.  PIK3CA T876A   NM_006218:c.2626A>G;p.Thr876Ala   49% WGS, 44% WES. This is a missense alteration in which an A is replaced by a G at coding nucleotide 2626 and is predicted to change a Threonine to an Alanine at amino acid codon 876. Classification Criteria   PM2 – Moderate.  Absent from controls (or at extremely low frequency if recessive) in Exome Sequencing Project, 1000 Genomes Project, or Exome Aggregation Consortium. BP4 – Supporting.  Multiple lines of computational evidence suggest no impact on gene or gene product (conservation, evolutionary, splicing impact, etc.)

Targeted Sequencing Findings: Whole genome, whole exome and transcriptome analysis of the patient's tumor sample is POSITIVE for the pathogenic and likely pathogenic variants described below. This tumor specimen has alterations in TP53 and SETD2, bi-allelic deletion of CDKN2A and CDKN2B, gain of MYC, EGFR, PDGFRA, and FGFR1, as well as a complex karyotype with several chromosomal gains, losses, and complex ploidy alterations. SNVI - TP53 R273H.  NM_000546.5: c.818G>A; p.Arg273His.  87% WGS, 94% WES, 100% RNA.   SETD2 frameshift deletion.  NM_014159.6: c.5374del; p.Ser1792ValfsTer10.  21% WGS, 24% WES, 47% RNA. Note: The TP53 variant R273H is present in a very high proportion of the sequencing reads due to a chromosomal deletion event in 17p involving the other copy of TP53 in tumor cells. Tumor Ploidy - This sample has a complex karyotype with near tetraploid cells, and multiple segments of gain, loss, and complex ploidy alterations. The copy number variants listed below are a representation of the changes considered to be clinically relevant. All ploidy changes described in this report are relative to a tetraploidy reference. COPY NUMBER VARIANTS - CDKN2A -  DELETION.  4 copy loss; whole gene    NM_000077.4.  9p21.3(20819836_24136628)x0.  CDKN2B - DELETION.  4 copy loss; whole gene    NM_004936.3.  9p21.3(20819836_24136628)x0. MYC - GAIN.  1 copy gain; whole gene. This is part of a complex  ploidy alteration on chromosome 8.  RNA expression of MYC  is increased.  NM_002467.4.  8q23.3q24.3(114565159_141883520)x5.  EGFR - GAIN.  2 copy gain; whole gene.  NM_005228.4.  7p11.2(54825045_55479233)x6.  PDGFRA - GAIN.  4 copy gain; whole gene.  NM_006206.5.  4q12(54555304_58197900)x8. FGFR1 - GAIN.  3 copy gain; whole gene    NM_023110.2.  8p11.23p11.22(37094148_38795140)x7.  Chromosomal losses: chromosome 2; 7q (x2). chromosome 10; 10q. chromosome 11; 11p15.2-p15.4; 11q23.3-q25; 12q11-q24.21; 14q31.1-q32.33. chromosome 15 (x2); 16q; 17p (x2); 21q11.2-q22.3 (x2); 21q22.12-q22.3 (x2); 22q11.21-q12.2. chromosome Y.  Chromosomal gains: 7p (x2). Complex copy number alterations (comprised of a series of multiple complex chromosomal gains and losses of various amplitudes and lengths): chromosome 3; chromosome 4; chromosome 5; chromosome 6; chromosome 8; chromosome 9; chromosome 13 (16p); chromosome 18; chromosome 19; and chromosome 20. cnLOH: 12q24.21-q24.33, chromosome 22

Targeted Sequencing Findings: Whole genome, whole exome and transcriptome analysis of the patient's tumor sample is POSITIVE for the pathogenic and likely pathogenic variants described below. This tumor specimen has a complex genome with alterations in TP53, TOP3A, MAPK7, and RB1 (see Tumor Ploidy and Comments). SNVI - TP53 R248Q.  NM_000546.5: c.743G>A; p.Arg248Gln.  100% WGS, 96% WES, 92% RNA;  in a region of 1 copy loss.  CNV - TOP3A. AMP ~8 copy gain, entire gene.  NM_004618.4.  17p11.2(18170401_18535300)x~10;  Upregulated RNA expression of TOP3A observed in comparison with other solid tumor cases.  MAPK7.  AMP ~5 copy gain, entire gene.  NM_139033.2.  17p11.2(19140801_19323900)x~7;  Upregulated RNA expression of MAPK7 observed in comparison with other solid tumor cases.  RB1  DEL. 2 copy loss.   NM_000321.2.  13q14.2q14.3(49006766_51268664)x0;  Nested 2 copy loss of RB1, including a small deletion of exons 18-27, and larger 1 copy whole gene deletion. Tumor Ploidy - Chromosomal losses: 1q32.1-q44, 3p26.3-q26.2, 4q21.3-q32.2, 4q34.1-q35.2, 5q35.2-q35.3, 8p, 10q21.1-q26.3, 12p, 12q12-q21.33, 13q12.11-q21.31, 17p13.3-p13.1, 17q11.2-q21.1, 18q22.3-q23, 19p13.11-q13.43, 20.  Chromosomal gains: (4q13.3-q21.3)x5, (4q32.2-q34.1)x7, (5p15.33-p15.2)x2, 5p13.2-p12, 13q21.31-q34 (with copy gain LOH), 18q22.1-q22.3, 19p13.3-p13.11, (Xp22.33-p22.31)x2, Y.  Complex Ploidy: 1q12-q24.2, 8q, 17p13.1-p11.2 (All 3 regions are with numerous complex structural variants and copy number variations of various sizes and amplitude).  cnLOH: 3q26.2-q29, 13q31.3-q31.3, 16q
GERMLINE: The following variant(s) were identified in gene(s) related to pediatric or hereditary cancer. TP53 - NM_000546.5: c.743G>A; p.Arg248Gln.  26% WGS, 21% WES (See Comments). Autosomal Dominant. Pathogenic.  TSC2 - NM_000548.4: c.2584G>A; p.Ala862Thr.  47% WGS, 52% WES.   Heterozygous.  Autosomal Dominant.  Uncertain Significance.  ELP1-NM_003640.4: c.1672A>G; p.Ile558Val.   56% WGS, 50% WES.  Heterozygous.  Autosomal Dominant / Autosomal Recessive.  Uncertain Significance.  AIP - NM_003977.3: c.949G>T; p.Asp317Tyr.  53% WGS, 51% WES.  Heterozygous.  Autosomal Dominant. Uncertain Significance.  TP53 c.743G>A; p.Arg248Gln, PATHOGENIC (See Comments). Pathogenic variants in TP53 cause Li-Fraumeni syndrome (Orphanet ID: ORPHA:524), a rare, autosomal dominant cancer predisposition syndrome characterized by the early-onset of multiple primary cancers including breast cancer, soft tissue and bone sarcomas, brain tumors, adrenal cortical carcinoma (ACC), leukemias, and other cancers. The TP53 c.743G>A (p.Arg248Gln) missense change has a maximum subpopulation frequency of 0.0026% in gnomAD v2.1.1 (https:// gnomad.broadinstitute.org/variant/17-7577538-C-T?dataset=gnomad_r2_1). The arginine residue is highly conserved across species and an established hotspot (PM1; PMID: 20182602,   33300245). This variant has a BayesDel score > 0.16 and Align GVGD (Zebrafish) is Class 35 (PP3). Additionally, transactivation assays show a low functioning allele, and there is evidence  of a dominant negative effect and loss-of-function (PS3; PMID: 12826609, 30224644). This variant has been reported in individuals with Li-Fraumeni syndrome, and individuals meeting the Chompret criteria (PS4; PMID: 1565143, 9242456, 15381368, 7887414). Finally, this variant was confirmed de novo in an individual with adrenocortical carcinoma (PS2; PMID: 15381368). In summary, this variant meets criteria to be classified as pathogenic based on the ACMG/AMP criteria, as specified by the ClinGen TP53 Variant Curation Expert Panel (PMID: 33300245): PS2, PS3, PS4, PM1, PP3.

Name: SJ071932

Clingen: This individual is heterozygous for a single base pair subsitution, g.153358G>T at intron19+05 of the RB1 sequence (Genbank L11910).  While this particular substitution has not been previously reported in the RB1 mutational database (http://www.d-lohmann.de/Rb), a G>C substutation at this same nucleotide has been reported as a splice site mutation.  Splice site prediction programs suggest that a G>T at this location would have a similiar effect to a G>C substutition, making it likely that this would affect splicing.
CLINGEN COMMENT: The patient is also heterozygous for a single base pair substitution, g.78216A>G in exon 17 of the RB1 gene.  This base pair substitution has not been previously report in the RB1 database, but would be predicted to be silent, p.R544R.  Six additional sequence variants were identified.  The patient is homozygous for g.39606T>C at intron03+45 and g.174438C>T at intron26+28; and heterozygous for g.56998A>T at intron07+35, g.73724A>C at intron12-29, g.156616A>G at intron19-77, all of which have been previously reported in the database as known polymorphic variants with no associated phenotype. Name: SJ030065
Clinical Presentation: 2 yr F with newly dx'd RB for enucleation
Targeted Sequencing Findings: Whole genome, whole exome and transcriptome analysis of the patient’s tumor sample for 565 cancer associated genes is POSITIVE for the pathologic or likely pathologic variants described below. This retinoblastoma specimen has a complex variant in RB1, which effectively ablates RB1 transcription, as well as MYCN amplification and a frameshift mutation in BCOR. Structural Variant - RB1Truncation - A complex rearrangement on chromosome 13 results in a large deletion [chr13:48880556-50698081], which causes heterozygous loss of RB1 past exon 1 (NM_ 000321) and homozygous loss of exon 2 [chr13:48880556-48885288].  Upon evaluation of transcription data, this complex structural variant effectively ablates RB1 transcript expression (only exon 1 is transcribed). SNVI - BCORL640fs   NM_017745.5:c.1918delC, p.(Leu640CysfsTer29)  [Allele frequency: 32% WGS, 29% WES].  This is a frameshift variant, in which a C is deleted at coding position 1918 and is predicted to change a Leucine to a Cysteine at codon 640, shift the reading frame and create a premature stop codon 29 amino acids downstream. Copy Number Variants - MYCNAMP (high copy gain).  A copy number variant in chromosome 2 [chr2:14632001-16279600] results in a high copy gain (>5 copies) of the entirety of MYCN (NM_005378).  This finding has been confirmed by FISH analysis. Tumor Ploidy - Chromosomal losses:  N/A. Chromosomal gains:  Partial: 1q, 6p

Clingen: This patient's peripheral blood DNA is heterozygous for a single base pair substitution, g.2111G>T in exon 1 of the RB1 gene (Genbank L11910). This variant would be predicted to results in a missense change, p.A18S and has not been previously reported in the RB1 database (http://www.d-lohmann.de/Rb) making it a variant of unknown significance.  Although there is no functional data, predictor programs suggest that this variant may be tolerated.  The patient is also heterozygous for another single base pair substitution, g.70243A>T in exon 12 of RB1. This substitution would be predicted to result in a missense change, p.T377S.  This substitution has been previously reported in the RB1 database and there is one report indicating that it is benign.
Targeted Sequencing Findings: DNA extracted from the tumor tissue is homozygous for a single base pair substitution, g.78280G>A in intron17+01 of the RB1 gene (Genbank L11910).  This variant has been previously reported in the RB1 database (http://d-lohmann.de/Rb).  It is predicted to alter splicing and is likely pathologic

Name: SJ071951
Clinical Presentation: 23 mth M with newly dx'd unilateral RB of the L eye
Clingen: DNA extracted from the patient's tumor sample is heterozygous for a single base pair substitution, g.59695C>T in exon 8 of RB1 (Genbank L11910).  This substitution would be predicted to result in a nonsense change, p.R255*.  The tumor DNA is also heterozygous for another single base pair substitution, g.156785C>T in exon 20 of RB1. This substitution would be predicted to result in a nonsense change, p.Q685*.  Both of these variants have been reported in the RB1 database (http://www.d-lohmann.de/Rb) and both are predicted to be pathogenic.
Targeted Sequencing Findings: A single base pair substitution was identified, g.78216A>G in exon 17 of the RB1 gene (Genbank L11910).  This single base pair substitution previously reported in the RB1database (http://www.d-lohmann.de/Rb) is predicted to result in no change to the RB protein (p.Arg544Arg) and is likely benign.  Five additional common polymorphisms were identified.  This patient is heterozygous for g.39573T>C at intron03+12, g.56998A>T at intron07+35, g.73724A>G at intron12-29; and homozygous for g.39606T>C at intron03+45 and g.174438C>T at intron26+28.  All of these variants have been previously reported in the database and have no associated phenotype.

Name: SJ058448
Clinical Presentation: 2 yr F newly dx'd with advanced RB L eye
Clingen: No RB1 mutations were identified in the DNA extracted from the patient's peripheral blood, however, three known polymorphisms were identified
CLINGEN COMMENT: This patient was identified as having a mosiac pattern for a 13q- abnormality by fluorescence in situ hybridization (FISH).  See Cytogenetics report xxxx for more details.  This large deletion would not be detected by the screening methodology performed in this study. Three polymorphic variants were found in the patient sample.  The patient's sample was homozygous for g.39606T>C at intron03+45 and g.174438C>T at intron26+28; and heterozygous for g.42068G>T at intron04+23 (Genbank L11910). All of these changes have been reported in the RB1 mutation database (http://www.d-lohmann.de/Rb) as known polymorphisms with no associated phenotype
GERMLINE: No numerical or structural abnormality was observed in the 20 evaluated metaphases by G-banding.  FISH using the RB-1 probe showed approximately eighty percent of the interphase cells evaluated were positive for the RB-1 deletion. In addition, FISH on metaphase plates showed that 17/20 (85%) metaphases had a RB-1 signal missing.  Thus, FISH showed a mosaic pattern with a deletion of 13q that was not observed by G-banding.

Name: SJ030043
Clinical Presentation: 5 yr F with new diagnosis of renal mass for resection
Targeted Sequencing Findings: Whole genome, whole exome and transcriptome analysis of the patient’s tumor samples for 565 cancer associated genes is POSITIVE for pathologic or likely pathologic variants described below. This report encompasses two Wilms tumor specimens from a left kidney resection, which are both described in surgical pathology report XXXXXXXX.  The dominant mass, which shows diffuse anaplasia (unfavorable histology), has multiple alterations, including single nucleotide variants in MYCN and TP53, as well as several changes in tumor ploidy/copy number, discussed below.  The smaller mass, a blastemal Wilms tumor with favorable histology, has no reportable alterations or copy number variants.  Specimen 1: Wilms tumor, dominant mass, diffuse anaplasia (Kidney, left, mid/lower pole mass). This specimen is POSTIVE for reportable pathologic or likely pathologic structural, single nucleotide, insertion/deletion and copy number variants described below. SNVI - MYCNP44L NM_005378.4:c.131C>T, p.(Pro44Leu)  [Allele frequency: 47% WGS, 51% WES].  This is a single nucleotide missense change in which a C is replaced by a T at coding position 131 and is predicted to change the amino acid at codon 44 from a Proline to a Leucine.  TP53C176G. NM_000546:c.528A>C, p.(Cys176Gly)  [Allele frequency: 76% WGS, 86% WES].  This is a single nucleotide missense change in which an A is replaced by a C at coding position 528 and is predicted to change the amino acid at codon 176 from a Cysteine to a Glycine. DICER1R821H.  NM_177438:c.2462G>A, p.(Arg821His)  [Allele frequency: 40% WGS, 42% WES].  This is a single nucleotide missense change in which a G is replaced by an A at coding position 2462 and is predicted to change an Arginine to a Histidine at codon 821. Copy Number Variants - WHSC1AMP (high copy gain). There are multiple areas of significant gene copy gain in chromosome 4p, as part of a chromothripsis event.  One such event [chr4:1155908-2002900] includes the entirety of WHSC1 (NM_133330), which results in a high copy gain (>5 copies) of the gene.  In addition, evaluation of the transcriptome data reveals that there is a high level of WHSC1 expression.  Tumor Ploidy - Full chromosomal gains: N/A   Partial chromosomal gains: 4p, 7q.  Full chromosomal losses: 17  Partial chromosomal losses:  4q, 7p.  Chromothripsis is identified at 4p.  The findings in chromosome 7 appear consistent with an isochromosome 7q [i(7q)].
GERMLINE: *Analysis of 63 cancer predisposing genes in this patient’s peripheral blood sample reveals a variant of uncertain significance in SMAD4.  No pathogenic or likely pathogenic variants are detected.* Data between *asterisks* in this section has been CORRECTED. Variants of Uncertain Significance - SMAD4P292L.  NM_005359.5:c.875C>T, p.(Pro292Leu)  [Allele frequency: 39% WGS, 47% WES].   This is a missense alteration in which a C is replaced by a T at coding position 875 and is predicted to change a Proline to a Leucine at codon 292.  Ploidy - No large (p or q arm) chromosomal gains or losses

Name: SJ030043
Clinical Presentation: 5 yr F with new diagnosis of renal mass for resection
Targeted Sequencing Findings: This report encompasses two Wilms tumor specimens from a left kidney resection, which are both described in surgical pathology report XXXXXXXXX.  The dominant mass, which shows diffuse anaplasia (unfavorable histology), has multiple alterations, including single nucleotide variants in MYCN and TP53, as well as several changes in tumor ploidy/copy number, discussed below.  The smaller mass, a blastemal Wilms tumor with favorable histology, has no reportable alterations or copy number variants.  Specimen 1: Wilms tumor, dominant mass, diffuse anaplasia (Kidney, left, mid/lower pole mass). This specimen is POSTIVE for reportable pathologic or likely pathologic structural, single nucleotide, insertion/deletion and copy number variants described below. SNVI - MYCNP44L.  NM_005378.4:c.131C>T, p.(Pro44Leu)  [Allele frequency: 47% WGS, 51% WES].  This is a single nucleotide missense change in which a C is replaced by a T at coding position 131 and is predicted to change the amino acid at codon 44 from a Proline to a Leucine.  TP53C176G.  NM_000546:c.528A>C, p.(Cys176Gly)  [Allele frequency: 76% WGS, 86% WES].  This is a single nucleotide missense change in which an A is replaced by a C at coding position 528 and is predicted to change the amino acid at codon 176 from a Cysteine to a Glycine.   DICER1R821H.  NM_177438:c.2462G>A, p.(Arg821His)  [Allele frequency: 40% WGS, 42% WES].  This is a single nucleotide missense change in which a G is replaced by an A at coding position 2462 and is predicted to change an Arginine to a Histidine at codon 821.   Copy Number Variants - WHSC1AMP (high copy gain).  There are multiple areas of significant gene copy gain in chromosome 4p, as part of a chromothripsis event.  One such event [chr4:1155908-2002900] includes the entirety of WHSC1 (NM_133330), which results in a high copy gain (>5 copies) of the gene.  In addition, evaluation of the transcriptome data reveals that there is a high level of WHSC1 expression.  Tumor Ploidy - Full chromosomal gains: N/A  Partial chromosomal gains: 4p, 7q. Full chromosomal losses: 17 Partial chromosomal losses: 4q, 7p.  Chromothripsis is identified at 4p. The findings in chromosome 7 appear consistent with an isochromosome 7q [I(7q)].  Specimen 2: Blastemal Wilms tumor, smaller mass (Kidney, left, upper pole mass). This specimen is NEGATIVE for reportable pathologic or likely pathologic structural, single nucleotide, insertion/deletion and copy number variants.  Tumor Ploidy - No large scale (p or q arms) chromosomal losses or gains.
GERMLINE: *Analysis of 63 cancer predisposing genes in this patient’s peripheral blood sample reveals a variant of uncertain significance in SMAD4.  No pathogenic or likely pathogenic variants are detected.* Variants of Uncertain Significance - SMAD4P292L.  NM_005359.5:c.875C>T, p (Pro292Leu)  [Allele frequency: 39% WGS, 47% WES]. This is a missense alteration in which a C is replaced by a T at coding position 875 and is predicted to change a Proline to a Leucine at codon 292.  Ploidy - No large (p or q arm) chromosomal gains or losses

Clinical Presentation: 3 mth M with newly diagnosed renal mass for resection
Clingen: No TP53 mutations were identified in the DNA extracted from the patient's peripheral blood, however, the patient is heterozygous for a single base pair substitution, g.11446C>G (Genbank NC_000017) that would be predicted to lead to a missence change, p.P72R. While this alteration is listed in the TP53 database (http://www-p53.iarc.fr) as a polymorphic variant, there have been some reports that associate it with an increased frequency of some cancers. The patient is also heterozygous for a 16 base pair insertion, g.11258insACCTGGAGGGCTGGGG  (p53PIN3) in intron 3.  This is listed in the TP53 database as a known polymorphic variant, but there are some reports that associate it with an increased frequency of some cancers (Oncogene (2004)23,1954-1956; Carcinogenesis (2010)31,643-647; Cancer Investigation (2008)26,168-172)
CLINGEN COMMENT: Three additional polymorphisms were identified in this sample.  The patient is heterozygous for g.11117C>G in intron 2, g.12273G>A in intron 4, and g.12803A>G in intron 6, all of which have been previously identified as known variants with no associated phenotype.

Name: SJ059665
Clinical Presentation: 2 yr M with newly diagnosed renal mass with metastatic disease for nephrectomy
GERMLINE: The following variant(s) were identified in gene(s) related to pediatric and/or hereditary cancer.  RAD51C. NM_058216.3:  c.34C>T (p.Arg12Trp). 48% WGS, 53% WES. Heterozygous. Autosomal Recessive/ Autosomal Dominant. Uncertain Significance.  RAD51C c.34C>T (p.Arg12Trp), heterozygous, UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE.  Interpretation Summary - Pathogenic variants affecting one copy of the RAD51C gene are associated with hereditary breast and ovarian cancer syndrome (OMIM ID: 613399), however they are thought to primarily increase susceptibility to ovarian cancer. Pathogenic variants affecting both copies of the RAD51C gene result in Fanconi anemia, a rare disorder that is characterized by developmental abnormalities, bone marrow failure, and predisposition to cancer (OMIM ID: 613390). The RAD51C c.34C>T (p.Arg12Trp) missense change has a maximum subpopulation frequency of 0.0080% in gnomAD v2.1.1 (https://gnomad.broadinstitute.org/variant/17-56770038-C-T?dataset=gnomad_r2_1). In silico tools are not in agreement about the effect of this variant on protein function, but to our knowledge these predictions have not been confirmed by functional assays. This variant is present two times in the FLOSSIES database which contains genetic variants from women older than 70 years of age who have never had cancer (BS2_Supporting; https://whi.color.com/variant/17-56770038-C-T). This variant was investigated in a case control study of 81 patients with head and neck cancer and 156 healthy control individuals (PMID: 24631219) and a study of 132 breast cancer cases and 189 healthy control individuals (PMID: 31905201), however it was not observed in any cases or controls. In summary, this variant meets criteria to be classified as of uncertain significance based on the ACMG/AMP criteria: BS2_Supporting

SNVI - CTNNB1 S45P.  NM_001904.3: c.133T>C; p.Ser45Pro.  50% WGS, 49% WES, 47% RNA.  ERCC6 Q723*.  NM_000124.3: c.2167C>T; p.Gln723Ter.  45% WGS, 41% WES, 20% RNA.  WT1 E324fs.  NM_000378.4: c.969dup; p.Ser324GlufsTer3.  100% WGS, ~100% WES, 100% RNA.  Tumor Ploidy - Chromosome number: 46.  Chromosomal losses: None.  Chromosomal gains: None.  cnLOH: 11p15.5-p11.2 (includes the WT1 gene).  Compared to the 2019 material (reported under SD-19-00709), this sample shows the same WT1 and ERCC6 variants as well as copy neutral loss of heterozigosity involving 11p, but it also shows the CTNNB1 S45P variant which was not identified in a previous analysis of the patient's inferior vena cava retro-hepatic segment specimen (SD-19-00709). The CTNNB1 T41A variant, the NF1 subclonal frameshift variant, and the 1p34 copy gain alteration detected in the 2019 specimen are not identified in this sample.
